title: Need Help with spices formula

posts.author: Abdulaziz (Abdulaziz Bakhsh)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi everyone,
need help with spices formula I have (a, b, c,) spices and each one is 500g and each time a flavor count is up by one reduce (a) and (b) by 20 and so on…
if anyone can help me with this formula I will be thankful.

and thank you for your time.
Abdulaziz

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Abdulaziz

More than happy to help. Can you restate your request in terms of your board and columns?

posts.author: Abdulaziz (Abdulaziz Bakhsh)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell
Thank you for your help.

For the columns.
Spice A | Spice B | Spice C | flavor count 1 | flavor count 2
500g | 500g | 500g | |

When (flavor count 1) is 1, 2,3…
reduce (Spice A) and (Spice B) by 20.

When (flavor count 2) is 1, 2,3…
reduce (Spice A) and (Spice C) by 50.

Thank you for your help.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Abdulaziz

I’m sorry it took me so long to get back to you.

I still don’t get exactly what you are wanting. I would need to know each of the column types. It seems like you are looking more for a transaction to change numeric column values and not an actual formula column.

To change numeric column values, there are 3 options:

  1. Use the General Caster app available in the marketplace.
  2. Implement a custom solution using Make/Integromat (or similar).
  3. Use a variation of this idea:
Count the number of times a specific column changes! Special workflows & use cases
Hi Everyone!! I’m Boaz, a Senior Customer Success Manager in the Enterprise team at monday.com. I work with large Enterprises and assist them with getting the most out of monday.com. TL;DR: I’m sharing a cool hack that allows you to use the combination of 2 automations, a date column and a special formula, to automatically keep track of the number of times a due date, or any column that you choose, is changed. What we are doing here is “tricking” the system using this combination of features…

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
